title: New DTCC PhD forum opens doors for collaboration
abstract: A new PhD forum with the goal of enabling collaboration, sharing research and strengthening the bonds within the centre has started in DTCC. The forum had its first kick-off workshop in March, which was described as eye-opening and valuable by participants.
summary: A new PhD forum with the goal of enabling collaboration, sharing research and strengthening the bonds within the centre has started in DTCC. The forum had its first kick-off workshop in March, which was described as eye-opening and valuable by participants.
intro: A new PhD forum with the goal of enabling collaboration, sharing research and strengthening the bonds within the centre has started in DTCC. The forum had its first kick-off workshop in March, which was described as eye-opening and valuable by participants.
body:
A new PhD forum is being launched within the Digital Twin Cities Centre to strengthen collaboration and exchange among doctoral researchers connected to the centre. The initiative, which will be held twice a year, aims to create a space where PhD students can share their research, learn about ongoing DTCC projects, and connect with other parties of interest in the centre.

“The idea is to bring the students together so they can better understand the different research projects within DTCC and find opportunities and ways to collaborate. Postdocs and project assistants as well as other people involved in DTCC will be able to provide valuable insight”, says Leonardo Rosado, leader and facilitator of the PhD forum and Director of Graduate Studies at the Division of Architecture and Civil Engineering.

The forum will enable PhD students to develop key skills needed during their doctoral studies, such as communicating research, reflecting on ethical aspects of research, and sharing knowledge with the wider academic community.

“There are a lot of aspects to research, like learning how to communicate it in a sufficient way, different ethical aspects and technology shifts. All these things will be discussed in these forums. Rather than senior researchers doing the talking, the PhD students themselves will be able to teach each other through active participation”, says Leonardo Rosado.

**Eye-opening first workshop**

The first workshop was held in March of 2026 and served as a kick-off event where participants introduced their research while getting to know more about DTCC’s activities and tools.

“It was great to meet researchers who share the same goal and vision as me. The day was very eye-opening, and it felt good to learn that while our research is slightly different, we still have a lot in common. I'm looking forward to continuing this work together”, says Ahmet Arif Yakupogullari, PhD student in the DTCC project Oversite.

The experience of cross-project value was shared by fellow PhD student Oliver Disney.

“Hearing how other researchers are working with digital twins in their projects was really valuable. My research focuses on the construction site, so it was great to hear perspectives at the broader city level and reflect on how different projects and technologies can complement each other. The PhD forum will be a great setting to continue these exchanges and learn from one another”, says Oliver Disney, PhD student in the Total BIM project.
